pairs.question: When was Crawford Plains neighborhood in Edmonton named and why?
pairs.answer: Crawford Plains was named in 1976 to honor Neil Stanley Crawford, a provincial cabinet minister and former Edmonton alderman, in recognition of his public service on the Edmonton Historical Board, Local Board of Health, and city council.

pairs.question: What percentage of residences in Crawford Plains were built during the 1980s?
pairs.answer: According to the 2001 federal census, 58.2% of the residences in Crawford Plains were constructed during the 1980s.

pairs.question: What types of housing structures are most common in Crawford Plains, and what percentage do they represent?
pairs.answer: Crawford Plains is predominantly composed of single-family dwellings (77%), followed by row houses (17%) and duplexes (6%).

pairs.question: What is the occupancy rate of owner-occupied homes in Crawford Plains?
pairs.answer: According to the 2005 municipal census, 84% of the residences in Crawford Plains are owner-occupied.

pairs.question: How is the educational infrastructure in Crawford Plains structured?
pairs.answer: Crawford Plains has a single school, Crawford Plains School, operated by the Edmonton Public School System.

pairs.question: What major shopping facility is located near Crawford Plains?
pairs.answer: Residents of Crawford Plains have access to shopping at the Mill Woods Town Centre shopping centre, which is located nearby.

pairs.question: What are the geographical boundaries of Crawford Plains?
pairs.answer: Crawford Plains is bounded on the west by 50 Street, the north by Mill Woods Road East and 16A Avenue, on the east by 34 Street, and on the south by Anthony Henday Drive.
